title: Human Nasopharyngeal Swab Processing for Viable Single-Cell Suspension
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6mboh","text":"A protocol for recovering viable single cell suspensions from cryopreserved human nasopharyngeal swabs for downstream applications, such as single-cell RNA-seq. The illustrated schematic below details the process.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dsscn","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"kr00","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"61B775E9341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":245,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":null,\"entityMap\":null}","mime":"image/png","nestedSelector":null,"original_name":"20200930_Nasal_Swab_Protocol_BioRender.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/public/827a38476558c180e8f2b20f00d29c6343ad50cab638a3a10b74a6a571aeb656/cmjvbggt7.png","shadow":false,"source":"https://content.protocols.io/public/827a38476558c180e8f2b20f00d29c6343ad50cab638a3a10b74a6a571aeb656/cmjvbggt7.png","width":550}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3fko2","text":"Samples should be collected by a trained medical professional using a nasal swab (FLOQSwabs, Copan flocked swabs) in accordance with the manufacturer’s instructions. Briefly, the process was performed as follows. First, the patient’s head was tilted back slightly, and the swab was inserted along the nasal septum, above the floor of the nasal passage to the nasopharynx until slight resistance was felt. The swab was then left in place for several seconds to absorb secretions and slowly removed while rotating. The swab was placed in a 1.7 mL cryovial containing 90% fetal bovine serum (FBS)/10% dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) and frozen using a slow-cooling device (Thermo Scientific Mr. Frosty Freezing Container) at -80 °C, and stored in liquid nitrogen.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4h9j0","text":"For hazard information and safety warnings regarding nasopharyngeal swabs or any listed materials, please refer to the SDS (Safety Data Sheet).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6vd0i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9k4pg","text":"For samples obtained from individuals diagnosed with, or at any risk of, an infection (e.g. SARS-CoV-2), additional precautions should be taken in accordance with your institute's regulations on biosafety. These include elimination of aerosol generating steps where possible (e.g., no vacuum aspiration), all steps prior to cell lysis should be carried out in a biosafety cabinet, including thermomixing and centrifugation where possible. When necessary, samples should only be removed from the biosafety cabinet in decontaminated and sealed secondary containment. Personal protective equipment including a gown, two pairs of non-sterile gloves, a protective surgical or N95 mask, and a face shield should be worn during sample processing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Rapidly thaw cryovial ( Tube A ) in hands or thermal block set to 37 °C . Carefully perform in accordance with your institute's safety guidelines. If handling potentially infectious material, inspect for cracks or leaks during warming
Remove swab from Tube A using clean forceps, trim swab handle using scissors if necessary.
Place swab in Tube B (15 mL conical), dip briefly to rinse swab.
Move swab from Tube B (15 mL conical) to Tube C . Proceed directly to step 23 for Tube C .
Transfer liquid in Tube A to Tube B (15 mL conical).
Using 1 mL, RPMI from Tube B (15 mL conical), wash Tube A .
Discard Tube A .
Centrifuge Tube B (15 mL): 400 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Remove supernatant with serological pipette.
Resuspend pellet in 1 mL, RPMI/10mM DTT .
Transfer suspended cells from Tube B (15 mL) to Tube B (1.5 mL). Discard empty 15 mL conical.
Place Tube B (1.5 mL) on thermomixer (37ºC, 300 rpm).
Incubate for 00:15:00 .
Centrifuge Tube B (1.5 mL): 400 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Remove supernatant with P1000 pipette.
Resuspend pellet in 1 mL, Accutase .
Place Tube B (1.5 mL) on thermomixer (37ºC, 300 rpm).
Incubate for 00:30:00 .
Place Tube C on thermomixer (37ºC, 300 rpm).
Incubate for 00:15:00 .
Place swab in Tube D . Proceed directly to step 31 for Tube D .
Centrifuge remaining liquid at 400 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes ..
Remove supernatant with P1000 pipette.
Resuspend pellet in 1 mL, Accutase .
Place Tube C on thermomixer (37ºC, 300 rpm).
Incubate for 00:30:00 .
Place Tube D on thermomixer (37ºC, 300 rpm).
Incubate for 00:30:00 .

After Tube B , Tube C , and Tube D have each finished their 30 minute incubations: In practice, we wait until all tubes have finished their 30 minute incubation in Accutase to synchronize. We leave tubes on incubation for a maximum 50 minutes.
Collect washing from Tube A in Tube B (15 mL conical).
Place 70 µm cell strainer in a 50 mL conical.
Wet cell strainer with 3 mL, quenching buffer (RPMI/10% FBS/4 mM EDTA) .
Pipette contents of Tube B , Tube C , and Tube D onto cell strainer. Do not discard tubes.
Use 1 mL, quenching buffer to wash each Tube B , Tube C , and Tube D .
Add quenching buffer from Tube washes to cell strainer. Discard Tubes B , C , and D .
Wash cell strainer with additional 2 mL, quenching buffer .
Discard cell strainer, cap 50 mL conical.
Centrifuge 50 mL conical 400 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes .
Remove supernatant with serological pipette.
Resuspend cell pellet in residual volume (often ~500 µL).
Wash 50 mL conical with 500 µL, RPMI/10% FBS .
Centrifuge Tube E 400 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Remove supernatant with P1000 pipette.
Resuspend pellet in 200 µL, RPMI/10% FBS .
Manually agitate the swab in Tube D in the quenching buffer to ensure full rinse.
Transfer washing from 50 mL conical to Tube E .
In 96 well plate, add 10 µL, trypan blue .
Add 10 µL, cells from Tube E to well containing trypan blue.
Pipette to mix cells in trypan blue, transfer 10 µL to hemocytometer port.
Count viable cells across 4 quadrants.
Record total cell number and calculate cell concentration.
Take photo of cells at 20x.
Load 20,000 viable cells on Seq-Well array. Or load total volume of Tube E if total cell count is lower than 20,000.
Label cryovials and add 100 µL, RLT/1% BME to each tube.
Add 20,000 viable cells to each tube. Tap or pipette to mix cell suspension with RLT/1% BME
Snap freeze on dry ice for at least 00:10:00 .
Transfer to -80 °C .
Prepare and label a 15 mL conical as Tube B with 5 mL, RPMI .
Prepare and label a 1.5 mL tube as Tube C with 1 mL, RPMI/10 mM DTT .
Prepare and label a 1.5 mL tube as Tube D with 1 mL, Accutase .
Prepare and label a 1.5 mL tube also as Tube B , leave this one empty.
Transfer resuspended cells from 50 mL conical to Tube E (1.5 mL tube).
creator: cziegler
